entry.title: Re: MDK Web + mobile app
entry.content:
The InitializeOffline.action defined in your MDK project is of type Action.Type.OfflineOData.Initialize which is applicable to mobile only. This action type won't work (and is not supported) on MDK web, which is why your MDK web doesn't initialize the service.

As we expanded MDK support for web some time ago, we introduced a new action type Action.Type.ODataService.Initialize that will work for both mobile (online/offline) and web (online only) platforms in a given MK project. This way, you can run the same MDK metadata on both mobile and web platforms(keeping web runtime limitations in mind). 

You can:

  • either update the _Type in your existing InitializeOffline.action (Change OfflineOData to ODataService).
  • OR use a platform function to call different actions on different platforms using $PLT(ios, android, web). 

For example:

 

 

"OnLaunch": ["$(PLT,/MDKApp/Actions/Service/InitializeOffline.action,/MDKApp/Actions/Service/InitializeOffline.action,/MDKApp/Actions/Service/InitializeOnlineWeb.action)"],

 

 

Hope this helps.

entry.title: Re: Failed to commit objects to server : Undefined Info S...
entry.content:
Hi Miroslav, did you find a solution ? I have the same problem after upgrade to SAP BI 4.3 SP4 Patch 400 ?

Best regards.

Lionel RISON

entry.title: Attachment Handling In SAP CPI| Send attachment to XI receiver
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

In this short blog, we will discuss attachment handling.

Our goal is to pick a random file, create an XML main payload from the file's properties, and send it to the receiver with the file content as an attachment.

 

The iflow looks like below.

In above flow, we will pick a random file from the FTP source directory and use LIP to implement the attachment logic.

 

Content modifier 

in this step, we will set Content-Type and FileTimestamp.

 

Groovy Script Set File content as Attachment

 

We will now use the standard script provided by SAP for attachment handling.

Note for your reference --> https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/create-attachments

 

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import org.apache.camel.impl.DefaultAttachment
import javax.mail.util.ByteArrayDataSource

def Message processData(Message message) {

    // Get content type
    def headers = message.getHeaders()
    def contentType = headers.get("Content-Type")
    
    // Define suffix
    def suffix = contentType.substring(contentType.indexOf("/")+1)
    if (suffix == "plain") {
        suffix = 'txt'
    }

    // Get the attachment name from the header
    def attachmentName = headers.get("CamelFileName")
    if (!attachmentName) {
        // Default to 'attachment' if the header is not provided
        attachmentName = 'attachment.' + suffix
    } else {
        // Ensure the attachment name has the correct suffix
        if (!attachmentName.endsWith('.' + suffix)) {
            attachmentName += '.' + suffix
        }
    }

    // Get message payload as input stream
    def body = message.getBody(InputStream.class)

    // Set MIME type
    def dataSource = new ByteArrayDataSource(body, contentType)

    // Construct a DefaultAttachment object
    def attachment = new DefaultAttachment(dataSource)

    // Add the attachment to the message
    message.addAttachmentObject(attachmentName, attachment)

    return message
}

 

You can adjust this script according to your requirements.

Groovy Script Set file Properties as payload

Now, we will use another Groovy script to create the payload as shown below:

 

<ns:Attachment xmlns:ns='namespace'>
<FileName> Filename </FileName>
<Directory> Directory </Directory>
<FileType>  Content_Type </FileType>
<FileSize> Content_Length </FileSize>
<FileTimestamp> TimeStamp </FileTimestamp>
</ns:Attachment>

 

 

 

To create these files, we are going to use the headers below:

CamelFileName   --> processed file name

CamelFileParent  --> Directory Name

Content-Type       --> Content-Type

CamelFileLength  --> File Size

FileTimestamp     --> FileTimestamp

Note->  You may need to add some header in runtime configuration --> allowed  header.

Here is the complete script to create the payload in the required format:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import groovy.xml.MarkupBuilder

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Retrieve headers from the message
    def fileName = message.getHeader("CamelFileName", String.class)
    def directory = message.getHeader("CamelFileParent", String.class)
    def fileType = message.getHeader("Content-Type",String.class)
    def fileSize = message.getHeader("CamelFileLength", String.class)
    def fileTimestamp = message.getHeader("FileTimestamp", String.class)
    
    // Log the retrieved headers for debugging purposes
    def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message)
    if (messageLog != null) {
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Debug - FileName", fileName ?: "null", "text/plain")
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Debug - Directory", directory ?: "null", "text/plain")
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Debug - FileType", fileType ?: "null", "text/plain")
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Debug - FileSize", fileSize?.toString() ?: "null", "text/plain")
        messageLog.addAttachmentAsString("Debug - FileTimestamp", fileTimestamp ?: "null", "text/plain")
    }
    
    // Create XML structure
    def writer = new StringWriter()
    def xml = new MarkupBuilder(writer)
    xml.'ns:Attachment'('xmlns:ns': 'namespace') {
        'FileName'(fileName ?: "")
        'Directory'(directory ?: "")
        'FileType'(fileType ?: "")
        'FileSize'(fileSize ?: "")
        'FileTimestamp'(fileTimestamp ?: "")
    }
    
    // Set the XML structure as the message payload
    message.setBody(writer.toString())
    
    return message
}

 


 Now, configure the Send and XI adapters, and deploy the integration flow. You will see the final output as shown below:

Here under Inbound Payloads you can see the main document and the attachment document containing the processed file content.

conclusion

 we've set up an integration flow that picks a random file from the FTP source directory, processes it using standard SAP scripts for attachment handling, and creates the main payload with specified headers. After configuring the Send and XI adapters and deploying the flow, the final output includes both the main document and the attachment document with the processed file content.


Feel free to optimize this solution further as needed, and I welcome any feedback or improvements 🙂.

entry.title: SEN: Error message "Call was rejected by callee"
entry.content:
Hi, when generating documents in EN, I get this error message "Call was rejected by callee". Producer will

stop working and I will need to restart Producer. This error only occurs when trying to generate documents in SEN. Has anyone had the same or a similar issue? Awaiting your valuable suggestions.

Anderson CM.

entry.title: Tcode KP06 is not available in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
We need to access transaction KP06 in Cloud.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Tcode KP06 is not available in S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,


In S/4HANA Public Cloud the KP06 was replaced with "Import Financial Plan Data" application. Kindly check the app documentation below:

Import Financial Plan Data


Best Regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: New xP&A content & composites performance
entry.content:
Hi SAP,

We've just installed all the newest SAP xP&A business content on SAP Analytics Cloud, and notice that a lot of optimized design stories & composites are now being used, with advanced scripting & new navigation menu's.

I wanted to raise a question (or concern) about startup performance of those stories, because each story seems to take +35 seconds to load, and I've been trying this after multiple browser refreshes.

When looking into the "Performance Optimization" function (using ctrl+a) and after further analysis it seems to me that:
- loading the story from url into browser -> adds +- 17 seconds
- loading the initialization script (new NavMenu loading only) -> adds +- 8 seconds
- loading the initialization script (other stuff) -> adds +- 5 seconds

When clicking in the navigation menu, this will open a new story each time, so this will be +35 seconds of front-end waiting time for the users each time they move to the next step/action in their process flow.

My question is: Is this the expected 'optimized' user experience?
And is this considered the 'best practice'? Or did I miss something?

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 4
entry.content: @noravonthenen - Not sure about this message. Shall I check after sometime?

entry.title: Re: Consume an On-Premise HTTP service from ABAP in the Cloud (Scenario : SOAP Service)

entry.title: BRF+ in S/4 HANA Public Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Public Cloud Experts, 

We have developed multiple BRF+ applications in our current On-Premise Landscape for forms and other custom RAP applications . Now we are looking at moving some of our custom to Cloud . When we build Custom BRF+ applications using the BRF+ application in the starter tenant and when i try to call the BRF+ application using the class cl_fdt_function_process class the get the error that the class is not permitted in the cloud .

Can you anyone guide me how to access BRF+ applications from Public cloud Developer Extensionsibilty ?

 

Thanks,

Vijaya

entry.title: Re: Validation using a matrix table - SAC Analytics Story
entry.content: Hi @Deb, there is no way to upload from excel file. You can either create a member type rule, copy & paste from excel; or create an attribute type rule from dimension attribute and upload from excel to dimension attribute.

entry.title: How to display Fiori Apps under roles in GRC 12 User Access Review (UAR)?
entry.content:
Hi Community,

In a Fiori first S/4 system, how do we display Fiori apps in the SAP GRC 12 User Access Review screen where they click to display Actions (transactions) under a specific Role?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to change the filter in Manage Sales Orders?
entry.content: Please use extensibility option and try to add a custom field for the sales order

entry.title: Re: SEN Signavio Missing Attribute error
entry.content:
Hi Prashant,

Once you have created the attributes in Signavio to house the SEN data there is no need to create them again.  I suspect that is why you get the error when you click the Create and Reload button.  The real issue is why you are even getting the error message in the first place.  The issue could be related to the integration or to a change in the browser so I would raise a ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-PRO and get someone to investigate.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: S1Q: Not able to generate tablespace alert
entry.content:
Hi Team,

we are performing alerting set up for DB2 tablespace 

we have identified 2 metrics 

1-Tablespace fill ratio

2-Tablespace maximum size

We have tried above two metrics and also tried Tablespace maximum size metric but we are not able to get exact values for tablespace sizes

Can you please suggest can we go with above mentioned template/metrics and perform any setting corrections? 

Thanks.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: S1Q: Not able to generate tablespace alert
entry.content:
Hello

Related to table space, in standard metrics/templates there is no mechanism to getting exact values for tablespace sizes for DB2 database.

You can create a custom metric but this falls under a consulting request KBA 83020 - What is Support – What is Consulting: On Premise Solutions.

Please check the KBA: 2484872 - Requested metric not included in standard templates

entry.title: Re: Enable Now Web Assistant is covering the application...
entry.content:
Hi Anna,

I assume that we are talking about SAP Companion here and not SAP Companion for Desktop running as SAP Companion.  If we are talking about SAP Companion for Desktop, the SAP Companion for Desktop toolbar will cover the right edge of the application because it sits as a separate layer over the application.

If we are talking about SAP Companion, when you open SAP Companion the application screen should slide to the left to leave space for the SAP Companion toolbar but this does not always happen.  I suspect that it is an integration issue in most cases where it does not happen.  You have two options:

1. If it's SAP Companion running in an SAP portal, e.g. S/4HANA, raise a support ticket using the Component ID KM-SEN-CMP and get support to take a look
2. Wait for the upgrade to SAP Companion, which will soon be available as a floating window.  Once SAP Companion is a floating window it can be moved to any part of the screen so will no longer be an issue.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇴 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (Bogota, Colombia

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇴 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Bogota, Colombia)

entry.title: Authentication with SAP BTP while developing
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I'll try to be as brief as possible - I have a front-end and back-end applications that i'm developing in SAP Business Application Studio.

Back-end is a node.js server using the express npm package.

Front-end is a fiori application.

I am utilizing "async await fetch" in my Fiori Controller Javascript to GET and POST from the Node.Js express server.

I have applied necessary CORS configuration to the Node.Js express server.

I run both applications from the terminal in Business Application Studio. I can successfully GET the endpoint on the back-end and retrieve results in the browser (because i'm logged into my S-User and authenticate with BTP).

However, when my front-end app does the GET request, I get 401, so I add Authentication header with Basic + base64string with username:password. Then I get CORS error.

Kindly explain the method for authenticating between two apps while developing in the BAS?

I've attached code screenshots.

Much Appreciated,

Reinhardt

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Trial system options for Private Cloud and On-Premise
entry.content:
When you are planning to move or upgrade to the latest SAP S/4HANA release, nothing beats getting hands-on experience in a real system.  It’s the perfect opportunity to put your self-enablement learning into practice to explore new processes, new tools, new concepts, and try out the latest key user extensions, and Clean Core development techniques.

However, setting up your own sandbox can be a significant resources/effort/time hurdle, especially when you still skilling up your team. Just like your productive landscape, for a sandbox system you will need to work through installation, activation, configuration, and then add some sample data for your exploration.

Did you know? There is an easy alternative!

A pre-configured and fully activated SAP S/4HANA trial system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL). These trial systems even come complete with sample data, test users, and demo guides to get you started! 

Hint: You can alternatively use the SAP Fiori configuration only SAP S/4HANA trial system, if you want to use your own sample data.

SAP offers various SAP Cloud Appliance Library (CAL) templates for different purposes. These templates enable you to create your own trial and sandbox systems easily.  With the CAL templates you have full administrative rights either hosted in the cloud on your preferred Cloud Provider (such as AWS, Microsoft Azure or Google Cloud Platform) or even on your own on-premise hardware.

Yes! Trials come with a free trial period, and you can extend your trial if needed.

In this blog post you will learn:

  • How you can use your SAP S/4HANA trial system
  • What you get with the latest SAP S/4HANA trial systems
  • How to obtain a trial system from SAP Cloud Appliance Library 
  • Tips on how to use your trial system
  • Extending your trial system
  • FAQ

Personal note: In the SAP S/4HANA RIG we rely on these trials and we use them with many of our customers for bootcamp trainings, demonstrations, and even for writing blog posts like this one! In the right context, these trial systems are lifesavers!

 

How you can use your SAP S/4HANA trial system

As part of the SAP S/4HANA Customer Care program and Regional Implementation Group (S/4HANA RIG) I use these trial systems all the time, for:

  • Exploring new business roles, apps, features, and tools
  • Explore the latest best practice tooling for creating and managing custom business roles
  • View SAP delivered context-sensitive help, such as Quick Tours, Tutorials, Terminology explanations, Guided Tours and more
  • Creating GIFs, videos, courses
  • Crafting bootcamp exercises for our 5 Steps to Fiori bootcamp
  • Running customer demonstrations for our Customer Care customers
  • Checking what’s underneath SAP Fiori apps and embedded analytics – such as VDMs and CDS Views
  • Exploring key user extension options – i.e. some of the most popular on-stack Clean Core extensions
  • Trying out more advanced Clean Core development techniques, such creating SAP Fiori launchpad plug-ins using embedded ABAP Cloud
  • And of course.... writing blog posts like this one!

These are some of the ways customers and partners currently use SAP S/4HANA fully activated trial system:

  • Preparing your move to SAP S/4HANA – net new or system conversion.
  • Planning your upgrade to a new release – you can create trials for different SAP S/4HANA release and Feature Pack Stack (FPS) combinations
  • Exploration of roles, apps, features, context-sensitive help, administration tooling, and extension techniques
  • Demonstrating processes, apps, features, and extension techniques
  • Upskilling on new best practice techniques and tools – such as coming to our 5Steps2Fiori bootcamp
  • Proof of concept explorations, e.g. to try out intelligent experiences such as Situation Handling.

From a legal perspective, you may wonder what is allowed and what is not allowed in the appliance.

Simply put, the first 30 days of using an appliance are governed by an online Terms & Conditions form that you must accept during the appliance creation process.

These terms prohibit productive usage (including uploading respective data), service provisioning to 3rd parties (eg. for prototyping or trainings), and other cases that go beyond the pure evaluation and trial purpose.

After these first 30 days, you will need contracts with SAP for SAP S/4HANA and for SAP CAL, is essence allowing you to use the appliance as specified in the SAP S/4HANA contract.

IMPORTANT: Even under a regular SAP S/4HANA contract with SAP, we STRONGLY advise to NOT use the appliance as starting point for a productive installation, eg. by using the system or parts of it as development system.  Why? The appliance contains contents and settings that might create trouble in your customer-specific project eg. cross-client coding and configuration, irreversibly activated business functions, functions with a separate license, etc. More details about the Terms & Conditions when using an appliance can be found FAQ for Trial Offering (Appliance Templates).

 

What you get with the latest SAP S/4HANA trial systems

In the CAL you will find appliance templates, which you use to create your trial system appliance(s). 

The latest CAL appliance templates include:

  • the fully activated version on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 and
  • the SAP Fiori configuration only version on SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01.

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Fully Activated Appliance

Link to appliance template: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01, Fully-Activated Appliance

This appliance template contains SAP S/4HANA 2023 (SP01) with pre-activated SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA core functions, and further scenarios for Service, Master Data Governance (MDG), Portfolio Mgmt. (PPM), Human Capital Management (HCM), Analytics, and more. User access happens via SAP Fiori, SAP GUI, SAP HANA Studio, Windows remote desktop, or the backend operating system for full administrative access.

For more details refer to section What is the content & scope of blog post SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Create your... - SAP Community

What you get: A trial system with pre-configured Fiori setup and pre-activated SAP Best Practices, further configurations in other functional areas and various demo guides for immediate exploration.

SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (SAP Fiori configuration only)

Link to appliance template: SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01 (Fiori configuration only)

This appliance template contains a pre-configured and activated SAP S/4HANA Fiori UI in client 100, with prerequisite components activated as per SAP note 3411022 - Composite SAP note: Rapid Activation for SAP Fiori in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS01. User FIORI_USER is prepared accordingly

This CAL system is prepared (in client 100) with activated admin roles assigned to a user named FIORI_USER, including:

  1. Administrator
  2. Configuration Expert – Business Process Configuration
  3. Analytics Specialist
  4. Business Process Specialist

Benefit to customers and partners: You get a system with pre-configured Fiori setup and FIORI_USER to immediately start exploring. You create your own sample data for exploration, demonstrations, and training.

Timeline / Availability of future CAL trial systems

Usually, the SAP Fiori configuration only release is published shortly after the official Release to Customer (RTC) of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Refer to: Planned Appliance Template Updates in 2024 - SAP Community

Usually, the Fully Activated release is published about 8 weeks after the official RTC of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Refer to: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Create your... - SAP Community

 

How to obtain a trial system from SAP Cloud Appliance library

Go to the https://cal.sap.com and select Explore Our Library to review the available appliance templates.

You can review details of which appliance templates are available for your Cloud Provider.  

Select an appliance template to review their contents and other useful information, for example, by reviewing the linked Getting Started Guide.

To create your own trial system from an appliance template, you will need to login to the CAL site at https://cal.sap.com.

Creating a new user is easy! Refer to Getting Started with the Cloud Appliance Library.

You will also need to create a CAL Account using your Cloud Provider subscription details.  

IMPORTANT: You can have different CAL users responsible for the Account - the Account Owner - versus responsible for your trial system(s) - the Appliance Owner.  This can be useful when Cloud Provider subscription details are centrally managed by your organization. Your Account Owner can control which appliance templates can be selected by an appliance owner when creating an appliance.

Then select Create Appliance to start the simple process to create your own trial system from the appliance template. 

 

You can refer to the following videos with step-by-step examples of creating your appliance on your Cloud Provider:

  • For AWS - SAP Cloud Appliance Library: creating an instance in AWS
  • For Azure - SAP Cloud Appliance Library: Creating an instance in Azure
  • For GCP – SAP Cloud Appliance Library: Create an Appliance in Google Cloud Platform

Tips on how to use your trial system

These tips come from our experiences with using trial systems ourselves and with customers on customer bootcamps.  

  • Accessing your trial
  • Security requirements for accessing your trial system
  • Preparing for demonstrations
  • Managing Cloud Provider costs

Accessing your trial

You can access your trial via the following options:

  1. The provided Remote Desktop option.
  2. Local Host (option of applying your own domain and host name)

The provided Remote Desktop is useful if only 1 or 2 people will use the trial system.

If you intend to use your trial system with a larger team, use the Local Host option. With the local host approach, multiple people can concurrently access your trial system. For example: 

  • Business users can login to your trial system’s SAP Fiori launchpad directly from their web browser
  • Administrators and functional configuration experts can login to SAP GUI using SAP Logon
  • Developers can login to your trial system from ABAP Developer Tools for Ecliipse

IMPORTANT: If using the local host option, you should consider selecting the Static IP address option when creating your appliance.  This ensures the IP address is the same whenever you suspend/resume your CAL appliance.

If you don't want your users to have to map the local hosts file, then you can optionally apply your own fully-qualified domain name (FQDN). This is the most convenient access for a larger group of business users  however, you will usually need the help of your IT organization.

Security Requirements for accessing your Trial System

Your trial system is available on the internet. 

You can find details of the ports and protocols needed along with other security aspects in the Getting Started guide for your appliance - section Security Aspects in Cloud Appliance Library. 

Preparing for Demonstrations

There are many demonstration guides provided for the fully activated appliance trials.  Refer to: SAP S/4HANA Fully-Activated Appliance: Demo Guides

Managing Cloud Provider Costs

You can use the Calculate Costs button to estimate the costs based on your Cloud Provider and Region.

In general, storage costs are minimal, as these are charged per month.

Activation costs are per hour. This means that while they may appear small, if you leave your trial instance constantly active they can quickly add up.

For example, you can calculate estimated costs for using your trial system for a 4 week (20 business day) bootcamp, assuming access for 8 hours per day, as follows:

Total Costs = Storage Costs (for 1 month) + Activation Costs (for 20 days x 8 hours per day)

To minimize costs, you can either:

  • Activate and suspend by schedule as part of setting up your appliance, or
  • Manually suspend and resume to minimize your activation time to only when you need it

Once you have created your appliance, you can Edit your appliance to change between scheduled and manual suspension at any time. 

With Activate and suspend by schedule you nominate when your trial system will be available.  You can nominate a once-off, daily, weekly, or advanced schedule for your time zone.  For example, you can make your trial system available Mondays to Fridays between 08:00 to 17:00. 

With Manually Suspend and Resume, you assign trusted responsible person as an operator to manually suspend your appliance instance when you are not using it, and resume your instance whenever you need it again. You can resume your instance whenever you need to.

When you resume your instance, you can optionally nominate to automatically suspend it a future time in hours, e.g. 4 hours from activation. You can Edit your active instance to adjust shorten/extend the current timeline.

If your instance is currently active and you need to extend the suspension time, you can Edit your instance to update it.

Select the “?” Help icon for your appliance template or instance for details on what else you can control.

 

Extending your trial system

Your free trial period per appliance template starts when you accept the terms and conditions for the appliance template.

If you need to extend your trial system beyond the 30-day free trial subscription, you can use your own SAP product licences or a SAP CAL subscription.   Refer to:  How to Keep an Expiring Trial Appliance in SAP Cloud Appliance Library

Hint: For bootcamps, if you want to create one appliance template briefly as a connection test, you can subscribe to a similar appliance template for an earlier SAP S/4HANA release. This is useful when you need to check your firewall and port settings will permit your team to access your appliance.  You can then subscribe to your target appliance template just before the bootcamp to make the most of the free trial period. 

 

FAQ

What product versions are included in a trial system?

Your trial system includes the relevant components for the selected SAP S/4HANA release and Feature Pack Stack (FPS), including:

  • SAP Screen Personas – for simplifying GUI transactions
  • SAP S/4HANA
  • SAP S/4HANA Foundation
  • SAP Fiori Front-end Server for SAP S/4HANA – i.e. embedded FES
  • SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – i.e. installation of the SAP Fiori apps
  • ABAP Platform for SAP S/4HANA

 

Becoming a SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA guru

You’ll find much more on our  SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA topic page

Other helpful links:

  • See all questions and answers about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA 
  • Follow SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA for more blogs and updates 
  • Ask a Question about SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG and Customer Care team.

entry.title: How to perform Arithmetic operations in Subscription billing?
entry.content: For usage based cases, while designing price element specification, after deriving the price from look up table, I want to multiply it with another factor( no of days which is received in input). How this can be achieved?

entry.title: Backlog found in Business Key Figures Chapter
entry.content:
As per report we want more details regarding the alert. Please help us to get more details on the issue.

"Backlog found in Business Key Figures Chapter "


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Backlog found in Business Key Figures Chapter
entry.content:
Hi customer,

The Red alert “"Backlog found in Business Key Figures Chapter " is rated for chapter “6.1 Reference Key Figures Measured Value Summary”. See below rating details.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Business Area: Key Figure Short Name =>Plant Maintenance: Overdue Notifications [K67]
Findings: 169.904 notifications were identified that are overdue by more than 30 days (15.386 less than three months & 138.263 older than twelve months). Based on 142 created (max per week) (GREEN[<142]; RED[>710]).
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Please kindly note that the values in this chapter originated from key figures executed in the your corresponding backend system. EWA reports just reflect the certain business related figures.
So the required action/analysis needs to be done by your corresponding business related area.
However, you may please refer to this KBA for further guidance about specific key figures 3401587 - How To Find More Information About a Business Key Figure Alert in the EarlyWatch Alert (EWA)

Thank you for your nice understanding and cooperation.

Best regards,
Yuki
SAP Support

entry.title: SAP CPQ 和 VCP 集成时，为何变式定价码的有效条件记录未展现在 Responder 中？
entry.content:
本文要谈论的主题是 SAP CPQ 通过 SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing（以下简称“VCP”）集成 SAP ERP (A)VC 时，变式定价码的定价条件记录显示问题。

根据 Pricing | SAP Help Portal，变式定价码的定价条件记录应该可以在 Configurator 页面的 Responder 中逐条列出。

然而，许多使用者反映，尽管完全遵循 SAP Help Portal 中的设定步骤，本应在 Responder 中显示的变式定价码的有效条件记录却没有展现出来。这究竟是为什么呢？

通过与 SAP Ticket 的沟通，我们得知了 SAP CPQ 产品经理对此功能的设计思路：能在 Responder 中展示的变式定价码定价条件记录，需要在 SAP VCP的Stateless Pricing API 的回传报文中满足以下三个条件：

  1. VarCond == true
  2. InactiveFlag in {' ','W'}
  3. Statistical = false

其中，第一个条件表示该定价记录为基于变式定价码的定价；第二个条件反映了定价记录的有效性；第三个条件表示在定价过程中，该定价条件设定为非统计类型。

以上为当前产品的设计，如果您的业务需求与之不同，欢迎您在 SAP CPQ (Configure Price Quote) - Customer Influence (sap.com) 上进行反馈。您的反馈可能会影响SAP的产品和服务的未来发展，我们感谢您的投入和支持。

entry.title: Variant Condition Records NOT Displayed in the Responder when SAP CPQ is Integrated with VCP
entry.content:
The main subject of this article is the issue of displaying variant condition records in Responder when SAP CPQ integrates with SAP ERP (A)VC through SAP Variant Configuration and Pricing (VCP for short).

According to Pricing | SAP Help Portal, pricing records of variant condition keys should be listed one by one in the Responder of the Configurator page.

However, some users may find it that even the integration has been set up following the guidance in SAP Help Portal, pricing records of variant condition keys are not displayed in the Responder. Why is this?

Through communication with SAP Tickets, we learned the design idea of the SAP CPQ product manager for this function: To display variant conditions in the Responder, the following three conditions must be met in the response payload of SAP VCP Stateless Pricing API:

  1. VarCond == true
  2. InactiveFlag in {' ','W'}
  3. Statistical = false

The first condition indicates that the pricing record is based on the variant condition key; the second one reflects the validity of the price condition record; and the third one means that the condition type is set to non-statistical type in the Pricing Procedure.

This is the current product design. If your business needs are different, you are welcomed to provide feedback in SAP CPQ (Configure Price Quote) - Customer Influence (sap.com). Your input may impact the future development of SAP's products and services. We appreciate your support and contribution.

entry.title: converting XML fields into single json string
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

My scenario : proxy <--> PI <---> rest         mode of communication : Synchronous

i have a requirement, i am getting 3 xml fields as shown in the below  in the input payload need to convert them as a single json string. could you please help on this to write an XSLT code. i am new to this xslt code.

input fields : 

client_id

client_secret

grant_type

 

Thanks in advance.

 

Best Regards,

Nagaraju

entry.title: Re: How to Construct a Deep link URL to navigate to Custo...
entry.content:
Hi Praveen,

Hope you are doing well.

I am sorry to tell you that C4C does not support this. C4C uses server-side rendering(SSR), meaning we cannot decide which part to render by deep link. (You may refer to the blog to know the basic concept of SSR Server Side Rendering in JavaScript)

You may also have been aware that the URL does not change when navigating to different tabs. This means that the URL cannot control the active tab. 

Please let me know if you have further queries on this topic.

Regards,
Alan

entry.title: Re: Can't see tickets
entry.content: Hi Rehanna, could you provide a screenshot to show case the issue here? Thanks!

Thanks for the reply. It's available now.

Best regards,

Isyraf

entry.title: SAP BAS debugger showing unbound breakpoints
entry.content:
Hi,

When executing SAP BAS debugger, my breakpoints are unbound and are skipped but statements are being executed. Can you please suggest if I am missing something. 

launch.json:

    {
      "command": "cds serve --with-mocks --in-memory?",
      "name": "cds serve",
      "request": "launch",
      "type": "node-terminal",
      "skipFiles": [
        "<node_internals>/**"
      ]
    }

entry.title: Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correct and available -4009
entry.content: Hello expert: I use Java to connect to SBO on the server through local DI-API. It used to be normal for a while, sometimes when I tried to connect, it showed login failure to the SLD server. Last time I tried to log in successfully by deleting and then adding the SLD service, I recently encountered this issue again. After following the previous steps, I found that it does not work. Then I tried to restart and modify the SLD address in b1's local machine.xml, but found that it didn't work either. Then, I copied the SBO b1 local machine.xml file that could run normally on the server to the local server, and the same problem still exists. My SLD service has two options: server IP address and computer host name. Do you have any experience or solutions in this area? The version I am using is sbo10.0 version

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 1: How to Enable Recommendation
entry.content:
As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402, you can complete sales order data with the aid of system recommendations in the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

Currently the following sales order fields support recommendation:

LevelField
HeaderSales Office
HeaderSales Group
HeaderCustomer Payment Terms
HeaderShipping Conditions
ItemPlant
ItemShipping Point

Before using the recommendation feature, complete the following steps:

 

Step 1: Activate Scope Item 73P

Note: Scope Item 73P has some region limitation. For details, please check Note 3389633 - Restrictions for Setting up Sales Order Automation (73P).

Refer to the following information to activate scope item 73P.

Type Reference Guide
Solution Builder managed (2SL)Link 1
CBC managed (2SL)Link 2
CBC managed (3SL)Link 3

To check whether 73P has been activated, you can execute prerequisite checks in the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470).

  1. Log on to your system with role SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST.

  2. Open the Intelligent Scenario Management app, and search for intelligent scenario "SLS_DOC_AUTO_COMPLTN".

  3. Click "Prerequisite Checks".

  4. Go through all steps and click "Start Prerequisite Check Run".

  5. Wait until the Overall Run Status becomes "Successful".

  6. Open check results. The third check indicates whether scope item 73P is activated in your system.

    Note: The first check indicates whether your system could connect to the Data Attribute Recommendation service. The second check indicates whether your system has enough transactional data for model training. Without enough transactional data, you still can start a training but possibly with poor quality.

 

Step 2: Activate Feature Toggle FTGL_SDACMONITOR

Solution Builder managed S/4HANA Cloud system

  1. Log on to your system with role SAP_BR_BPC_EXPERT.

  2. Open the Manage Your Solution app (F1241), and click "Configure Your Solution".

  3. Search for feature toggle relevant configuration.

  4. Activate FTGL_SDACMONITOR.

CBC managed S/4HANA Cloud system

  1. Log on to your CBC system.

  2. Search for feature toggle relevant configuration.

  3. Activate FTGL_SDACMONITOR.

 

Step 3: Train Machine Learning Model

  1. Log on to your system with role SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST.

  2. Open the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470), and search for intelligent scenario "SLS_DOC_AUTO_COMPLTN".

  3. Click "Train" to start a new training.

    • Version: Choose "3.0". This field indicates the API version that you use to start a training. Currently only "3.0" is available.
    • Package Size: This field indicates the size of uploading batch. If your system doesn't have enough memory, specify a smaller number, and vice versa.

    You can customize your training data in this dialog. Please see the next blog for more details.

  4. Wait until the training status becomes "Completed".

  5. Select the training instance and click "Deploy".

  6. Wait until the deployment status becomes "Deployed".

  7. Select the deployed instance and click "Activate for Me" or "Activate for All".

Okay! Now you can use the recommendation feature in your system.

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 2: How to Customize Training
entry.content:
Training the machine learning model in the Intelligent Scenario Management app (F4470) is a prerequisite for using the recommendation feature. A training is based on historical transactional data in your system. In this blog, I'll show you how to start a training with your customized data.

Training Filters

In the "Training Filters" section, you can restrict training data based on particular conditions.

For example, to base your training on the data created between Jan 1 2023 to Dec 31 2023, you can set the CREATIONDATE filter.

Note: The condition option supports Between, Equal to and Not equal to.

After you start a training, you can check the filter settings and uploaded dataset size.


Training Details

In the "Training Details" section, you can specify your model description and configure target fields.

For example, if you want to enable recommendations only for field Plant, de-select other fields.

Once the training is completed, you can check training details about the target fields in the Debriefing section.

entry.title: Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Overview
entry.content:
The scope item 73P has been delivered as of SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402. Within the scope item, you can:

  1. Complete sales order data with the aid of system recommendations in the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app

  2. Monitor recommendation details and processing in sales orders using the Monitor Recommendations for Sales Document Completion app (F7515)

Note: SAP S/4HANA Cloud consumes the machine learning capabilities of the Data Attribute Recommendation service to predict sales document data.

Features

Complete Sales Order Data Based on Recommended Values

Currently the following sales order fields support recommendation:

LevelField
HeaderSales Office
HeaderSales Group
HeaderCustomer Payment Terms
HeaderShipping Conditions
ItemPlant
ItemShipping Point

Before using this feature, ensure that certain prerequisites are met. Then you can test the feature as follows:

  1. Open the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

  2. Create a sales order with at least one item.

    Note: The machine learning model is trained on sales order item level, so your sales order must contain at least one item.

  3. Click the "Incompleteness Info" button in the sales order header.

  4. Navigate to a target incomplete field from the message popover, or click on a target field to view the recommendations.

Monitor Recommendation Details and Processing in Sales Orders

You can view all sales orders with recommendations in the Monitor Recommendations for Sales Document Completion app (F7515).

List Page

You can choose the "Complete Document Data" button to navigate to the Manage Sales Orders - Version 2 app.

When you click on a hyperlink in the "With Recommendations" column, recommendation details are displayed in a popover:

You can choose a "Log" icon to display the completion log of the sales order:

A sales order field with recommendations can have the following statuses:

StatusCase
Acceptance PendingThis field has recommendations, and it is empty now.
Recommendation AcceptedOne of the recommended values has been manually selected.
Entered ManuallyA value other than the recommended values has been manually entered.

 

Blog Series Navigation

Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 1: How to Enable Recommendation

Recommendation-Assisted Sales Order Creation - Part 2: How to Customize Training

entry.title: How can I add a new communication scenario to my SAP S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
Hi experts, I'm trying to add a new communication scenario to the 'Communication Arrangement' app in my SAP S/4HANA Cloud. Specifically, I'm trying to use the module SAP_COM_0120 related to the billing document, but it does not appear on the 'Create' page. Could you guide me on how to add this module typically? I've reviewed some documents and might need to adjust them from SAP Netweaver. Could you confirm if this is the correct procedure? Your assistance in this matter is highly valued and appreciated.

 

Here's the capture on my list.

I would like to know if it is related to Odata V2 (SOAP API) so I can't add it or if I don't have the privileges to establish this module with Communication Arrangement.

 

Attached is the API file description I need.

Overview | Billing Document - Read, Cancel, GetPDF | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Thank you!

Ankita

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot Not Generated despite the correct Mast...
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

Let me know check this with security team.

entry.title: Re: SAP BAS debugger showing unbound breakpoints
entry.content: The scripts in UI run in the browser. You need to set breakpoints in the browser (developer tools).

entry.title: Re: Inspection Lot Not Generated despite the correct Mast...
entry.content:
Dear Sir,

Thanks a lot for reply.

Please see response inline.

"inspection lots are generating sometimes and on other occasions it is not" depends what the "other occasions' are.

If another material -> Is this material active with the inspection type in the material? ---- It is the same material, inspection type is active. 

If inbound delivery is used -> inspection type with HU activated in the material master? ---- Inbound delivery is used, inspection type with HU is not  activated in the material master.

If another plant -> Is this plant also active for inspection? ---- Plant is same.

If the same material & purchase order -> Is the material setting 'Control InspLot' that each GR has it own inspection lot (and not one inspection lot per purchase order item, one inspection lot per material)? ---- Setting for 'Control InspLot' (Blank) For each material document item.

Hope that this inspires you to find the cause, but I assume it is master data related. ---- Currently ABAPer is debugging as per SAP KBA 3017532 inspection lot is influenced by customer exit.

entry.title: Given a date, how to find the nearest Monday using SAC Data Action
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Given a date in integer format, how to find the nearest Monday of it in SAP SAC data action. 

We have a date in interger format in a measure called: "Shipment Date", that will be entered by the planner, and we need to find the nearest Monday after 10 days, that's the max "Delivery by Date".

So, for example, Shipment date is: 05/27/2024,  the delivery date should be calculated as: 06/03/2024, instead of 06/10/2024, as this is the nearest Monday to date: 06/06/2024, when added 10 days to 05/27/2024. 

Thanks,

SP

entry.title: Re: Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correc...
entry.content:
Hi ZHD 

Please verify SLD address, for the file "b1-local-machine.xml"  from : C:\Program Files (x86)\SAP\SAP Business One DI API\Conf

If still having issue, you could try to reinstall DI API.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Re: use odata in sapui5 (Invalid Function Import Parameter )
entry.content:
Hello,

If you’re trying to access Function Import then it should be oModel.callFunction

Best Regards,

Viswanath

entry.title: MDK Custom Client 24.4.1 + iOS TestFlight Upload Failing - ITMS-90338: Non-public API usage
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are preparing our app for Apple App store distribution through XCode and to give it to our testers through TestFlight.

We have created a custom MDK Client 24.4.1 and use our certificates and provisioning profiles to package the app.

The builds are not available in TestFlight and an email is recieved stating:

Hello,

We noticed one or more issues with a recent delivery for the following app:

  • App Name
  • Version 1.0.0
  • Build 8

Please correct the following issues and upload a new binary to App Store Connect.

ITMS-90338: Non-public API usage - The app references non-public selectors in Frameworks/SAPMDC.framework/SAPMDC: setNumberOfRowsInSection:. If method names in your source code match the private Apple APIs listed above, altering your method names will help prevent this app from being flagged in future submissions. In addition, note that one or more of the above APIs may be located in a static library that was included with your app. If so, they must be removed. For further information, visit the Technical Support Information at http://developer.apple.com/support/technical/

Apple Developer Relations

 

Can you please assist?

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Analytics Cloud Release 2024.11
entry.content:
SAP Analytics Cloud brings together analytics and planning with unique integration to SAP applications and seamless access to heterogenous data sources. It helps everyone in your organization make decisions without doubt with trusted insights and integrated plans.

This blog post introduces the features of the SAP Analytics Cloud 2024.11 release available for SAP Analytics Cloud FastTrack customers. For SAP Analytics Cloud Quarterly Release Cycle (QRC) customers, this release and its features will be available as part of the QRC Q3 2024 release. View the QRC release schedule here.

 

System Administration and Technical Requirements

 

Although not required, you may upgrade to the latest SAP Analytics Cloud agent version 1.0.407 to take advantage of all data acquisition types.

The agent’s minimum version remains at 1.0.75, with the following exceptions:

  • Version 1.0.99 for SAP ERP/SQL Database
  • Version 1.0.365 for SAP universe (UNX). The SAP Analytics Cloud agent requires additional setup steps, for more details see SAP Note 3262098
  • Version 1.0.353 for SAP Business Warehouse (BW)
  • Version 1.0.235 for SAP HANA View
  • Version 1.0.248 for File Server

For more information, see System Requirements and Technical Prerequisites.

Regarding supported features and required updates for the integration of SAP BW with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2541557 for further details.

Regarding supported features and required updates for live universe access through SAP BusinessObjects Live Data Connect with SAP Analytics Cloud, refer to SAP Note 2771921 for further details.

Please download the latest version of the Simple Deployment Kit from Software Center.

Note: To avoid downloading older versions and potentially exposing users to a vulnerable bundling of the Cloud Connector with SAPJVM, or Apache Tomcat, only the three latest versions of the kits are available to download.

For Cloud agent version change management details, refer to SAP Note 3264839.

 

Release Highlights

  • Administration and Service Management
    • Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP
  • Data Exploration
    • Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer
    • Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer
  • Data Integration
    • Data Export API - Delta Difference
    • Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections
  • Story Design
    • Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides
    • Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations
  • Story Extensibility
    • Retrieving non-input-ready variables as dynamic text or through script API

 

Administration and Service Management

 

Prevent loss of SAP Analytics Cloud tenant Accidentally Deleted via BTP

If you accidentally delete your service instance in the SAP BTP cockpit, it can be restored within seven days. Learn more here.

 

Data Exploration

 

Add Calculations on Structure Members for two Structure BW Queries in Data Analyzer

A "+Add Calculations" button has been added in the Structure section of the Available Objects panel. This allows the creation of calculations using structure members in two structured queries on BW.

 

Persistence of last used values in Data Analyzer

The last used values for defined components and interactions are now kept so that when data analyzer is reopened, the options are the same. That way as a data analyzer user you can now set the following:

  • Show/hide the filter line by default
  • Open/close individually the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list 
  • Expand/collapse the styling panel and the builder panel & available objects list by default
  • Set the system and connection type by default in the data source browser
  • Keep all settings in the individual export dialogs for PDF, Microsoft Excel and CSV export (except for Export name)

 

Data Integration

 

Data Export API - Delta Difference

Data Export Service API subscriptions has been extended to support the calculation of the difference between old and new numerical delta values when performing a Delta Extraction, rather than just new measure values.

 

Support X509 certificate auth for OData-based Connections

Users can now select Client Certificate authentication and use an X.509 certificate for generic OData connections, SAP Business ByDesign Analytics and SAP S4/HANA Cloud connections.

 

Story Design

 

Optimized Story Experience now supports Export as Google Slides

The optimized story experience now supports export as Google Slides. Google Slides can be saved as a new presentation, or the slides can be added to an existing presentation in a Google Drive folder.

This is not supported in the lite viewer.

 

Lite Viewer now Supports Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension in Geographical Visualizations

The Lite Viewer now supports color by dimension for the bubble layer in geo maps. Bubble Layers with Color by Dimension will no longer cause an automatic redirect to the Full Viewer.

There are few improvements that have been made to the interactivity of Geographical Visualizations which includes:

  • Improved Interaction with Color by Dimension Bubble
  • Consolidation of Drill with Tooltip
  • Improved Threshold Tooltip

 

Story Extensibility

In the optimized story experience, dynamic text and variable related script APIs such as getVariables and getVariableValues now support non-input-ready variables.

Retrieve the variables that are not input ready, such as customer exit variables, from a live connection to SAP Business Warehouse (SAP BW):

  • As Dynamic Text
  • Through script APIs
    • getDataSource().getVariableValues()
    • getDataSource().getVariables

 

This blog post only provides a high-level overview of the latest features and enhancements of SAP Analytics Cloud. You can find more details on these features in your In-App Help Guide for FastTrack customers and see this SAP Knowledge Base Article if you’d like to learn more about FastTrack and Quarterly Release Cycle releases. 

If you have any questions regarding the content in this blog post, you can comment below. If you have product specific or technical questions, post a question to our SAP Analytics Cloud Questions & Answers forum. Visit our SAP Analytics Cloud Community pages to find more product information, best practices, and more. And check out our SAP Road Map Explorer to see more upcoming features of SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Re: Purchase requisition created in discontinued material...
entry.content: Greetings,

entry.title: Re: How to mark the top of the SAC filter panel
entry.content:
Hi heejujoung, 

  From your screenshot, seems you are in the new 'Present mode' of the story. I would suggest:

  • Still open in view mode by default. You can set it via story setting -> View time setting -> Navigation -> open default in view mode. 
  • Not showing filter panel by default in view mode. You can set it via story setting -> View time setting  -> Filter panel -> View time configuration -> Hidden .

Best regards, William

entry.title: Brands created in CSB is not appearing in Manage Data > MarketingBrand
entry.content:
Hello, 

I have created brands in CSB, however this is not reflecting in Manage Data > MarketingBrand. 

My user has permissions to object MarketingBrand. I have complete Odata refresh as suggested in one of the KBAs. 

Is there anything that I need to complete ? Any help on this will be great. 

Just additional information: This was configured in Development instance previously and it was working fine. 

Now while configuring in Test instance, I am not seeing the brands in Manage Data. This in turn blocks me from creating a business rule. 

Note: Unified Data model is not being used.

entry.title: Re: Purchase requisition created in discontinued material...

Greetings,

you can try setting MRP type to ND at material master MRP 1 tab.

Best regards!

entry.title: Re: Pricing based on custom attributes with SAP Subscription Billing
entry.content: That's very informative. Thanks for sharing. Is there any blog available to understand how to do mathematical calculations. basically, I want to take the price output from look up table and multiply with another parameter to get final amount. How to achieve this?

entry.title: Clean Core Approach for SAP Fiori Development
entry.content:
I am still a bit confused with clean core concept with regards with Fiori/UI5 development. I hope someone can enlighten me. 

For example we have a business requirement to extend/enhance a standard app.

There are a few ways on how to do this

1. Key User Adaptation. If the requirement can be satisfied via AdaptUI or creation of custom fields / logic using Standard Fiori Apps Custom Fields and Custom Logic. This I know is clearly clean core approach.

2. Component Configuration. We still do use this kind of extension specially for the old Fiori applications. 

Is using extension hooks considered as clean core? How about view replacement? controller extensions? controller replacements? Are these considered clean core approach? 

3. Controller Extension via Adaptation project. I believe this is also clean core approach right?

4. Creation of a z copy of the standard app and deploying it as a new app. App will either use a standard OData service, a custom OData service or a z copy of a standard OData service. Is this way clean core also?

entry.title: temu coupon code { acu866809 } 100% discount for all customers in 2024
entry.content:
Use temu coupon code { acu866809 } 100% discount and additional $100 off for all customers in 2024 if you are looking for a way to save money on your first purchase at Temu.
This exclusive code provides up to a 90% discount on select products, also gives 3 free items.
 

How to Get the 100% Off Coupon Code?

Sign Up: The first step is to create a Temu account. You can sign up using your email address or via your social media accounts.

Explore Offers: Once you're signed up, look for the $100 off promotion for first-time users. This offer might be directly visible on the homepage or under a specific ‘Deals’ or ‘Promotions’ section.

Follow below to apply this temu coupon code:
create account
add item in cart
Apply the Coupon: When you're ready to make your purchase, apply the coupon code “acu866809 ” at the checkout. This will give $200 off on your all items.

Conclusion:
make use of this code for best discounts and get $100 off.

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 4
entry.content:
Hi Nora,

entry.title: Re: Failed to connect SLD, make sure SLD server is correc...
entry.content: I have installed DI API on my server

My name is Magdalena, I'm from Siemianowice Śląskie. 

My hobbies include cycling, hiking and traveling. 

.... And I'm interested in ABAP.

I also have a question, are there possibilities, for people who are interested in starting a career in SAP, to engage with SAP professionals on this platform, to ask questions and for advice?

entry.title: Re: 🇨🇴 SAP Build: Create Apps and Processes Without Code (Bogota, Colombia)
entry.content: ¡Hola Juan Pablo! Excelente, nos vemos en el evento 🙂

entry.title: Re: Adobe forms - & character
entry.content:
If you are maintaining the text as Header Text, click on the 'Detail' icon at the bottom to launch the Text Editor. Re-write the sentence with &. This will appear as '&' in Adobe PDF.

 

Hope that helps!

entry.title: Re: 🇧🇷 Event-driven integrations with SAP Integration Suite, advanced event mesh (São Paulo, Brazi
entry.content: 👏 see you at the event!

entry.title: SAUG BTP Hackathon
entry.content:
During the build phase, the SAP team will assist and hand hold the teams to help them in the implementation of their SAP BTP use cases. SAUG and SAP will help you to work with SAP BTP and also help you build a working prototype for your business use cases. SAP’s team of global BTP experts will enable participating teams on the nuances of the implementation / usage of SAP BTP and will share SAP’s experience and knowledge. There will be 6 enablement webinars which will be deep dives of various BTP technologies and applications. 
 
 
Finalists will be invited to show off their projects in a Demo Jam at the SAUG National Summit in August. The winners will receive recognition and rewards and the whole community will benefit with outstanding learnings from real use cases.
 
We encourage members to send their submissions, nominate their teams and take part in the valuable knowledge building. This will be a great learning opportunity which will develop real-life BTP use cases and deliver direct business value to your organisation.

To know more details of the Hackathon and how to join, please refer to this webpage on SAUG Website

https://www.saug.com.au/Communities/SAUG_BTP_Hackathon

entry.title: Re: sap abap email functionality using classes issue with...
entry.content:
Hi vieet,

I would recommend use of String Templates: https://help.sap.com/doc/abapdocu_latest_index_htm/latest/en-US/index.htm?file=abenstring_templates.htm

Best regards,
Peter

entry.title: Re: Creating Custom Button and Event Handling in SALV (SAP List Viewer)
entry.content: Informative

entry.title: Re: Process order showing double component in materials tab.
entry.content: Hello Varad , In case of no further discussion required then you can close this thread and you can mark correct answer as best answer for credits so that it will be useful for others in future

entry.title: Re: Multiple Profit center issue in Delta accrual document
entry.content:
Hello Pankaj,

Before implementing any custom logic try this configuration:

Under Settlement Documents -> Settlement Document, find your delta accrual settlement document type and under the "Accounting" tab, change the setting "Partner Lines at Item Level" to "Yes". Then try to reprocess the delta accrual settlement.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Trigger a process in SAP Build Process Automation from SAP Build Apps without using API
entry.content:
Hi  @Isyraf_Afifi ,

Did you get a chance to look at section Retrieve process instances above ?

Do you need more info here ? If so, can you please tell where exactly you are struck ?

Thanks and Best Regards,

Rama.

entry.title: Re: Desktop Agent for MAC in SAP Process Automation.
entry.content: Looks like this extension has been removed now.

entry.title: Re: How to use Postman to call SuccessFactors API using OAuth authentication method and SAP Offline
entry.content:
Hello,

 

is there a tutorial for MAC please?

Thank you!

Best Regards,
Dagmar

entry.title: Re: Instead of customer account, Expense GL should hit fo...
entry.content:
Hi @trinath80 

I saw your post which says you want the accounting entries to be shown as:

Customer A/c Dr

Revenue A/c Dr

VAT Expense A/c Dr

         To Customer A/c Cr

         To Revenue A/c Cr

         To VAT Payable A/c Cr

If there a legal requirement for such a flow? Why would you show a FOC Sale to revenue?

Ideally, for FOC sale to calculate the tax, you would move tax condition after VPRS and than do a new condition type with 100% discount after the tax condition in your pricing condition. You could make the tax condition type as accrual so that it posts the tax as you want.

The below question answered in the past, might be of help for FOC Related pricing.

Solved: Free of charge item tax calculation - SAP Community

Thanks & Regards

Sanil Bhandari

entry.title: Re: Explore the New SAP S/4HANA Utilities Trial with Four Guided Tours
entry.content:

where can I find the four guided tours, please? The links in Getting Started Guide are not valid and our CAL is more then 30 days after deployment.

Thank you, best regards.

Pavel Paulus

entry.title: Re: Given a date, how to find the nearest Monday using SA...
entry.content:
Hello @SP9 

I doubt that you will find a good solution for that in SAC.

But you could maybe testing if you can utilize the Date's Day name colunm:

I'm not sure if you can access this... but even if it might become a complex logic?!
Good luck,

Martin

entry.title: Re: WIReportServer.exe consuming all memory + ERR_WIS_30270
entry.content: I had the same problem today on 4.3 SP04.300 - solved it by restarting the webintelligenceprocessing servers on the node having the problem

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 4

entry.title: Re: How to use OpenTelemetry for SAP Cloud Foundry / Clou...
entry.content:
Hello BarcDe,

I am doing a Proof of Concept to explore OpenTelemetry of SAP Cloud ALM for BTP Cloud Foundry environment, for example, to monitor custom application deployed on it.

I reached to following SAP Documentation but I did not understand what exactly is to be done. Perhaps you could find it useful:

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/data-collection-infrastructure.html

I have another question open here on community.sap.com.

From your question, I realised some other steps which were not documented, at least I did not find it.

Would it be possible for you to share stepwise procedure which you described?

  1. Inserted the destination.
  2. Created a service key in the CALM subaccount.
  3. Set up the Alert Notification Service in the CF subaccount.
  4. Added the Cloud Foundry subaccount to the scope selection.
  5. Activated monitoring.

Warm regards,

Amey

entry.title: Receiving material using 109,need to return material using 122
entry.content:
Hi ,

  Usually we have received material using 107 / 109 movement type for PO. After receiving material we want to return partial qty to vendor because of poor quality. What is the procedure I have to follow for this?

Any tips, please.

Regards,

MI

entry.title: Re: Time Off Recruit Rule: Rounding up in 0.5s
entry.content:
Hi Sorai,

I did in fact find that previously but I was looking to round up as to down, and struggled anyway to replicate it.

Best regards,

entry.title: Re: May Developer Challenge - AI Services - Week 4
entry.content: Sure, let me try again.

entry.title: Re: Date shown wrong in SAP Analytics Cloud based on SAP...
entry.content:
Hello @tilted 

this is a well-known missing feature (for German Datasphere users) that SAP does not provide an in-built function to display a German date format 😪

What you see here is the internal key for the dates, like stored on the database.

We (German users) have to create our own date format in an additional (text) view... which you associate to your date field... then in the SAC story you can display the text as dd.mm.yyyy, and the key is still the internal format.

Formula: CONCAT(Tag,CONCAT('.',CONCAT(MONTH,CONCAT('.',YEAR))))

BR, Martin

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the SAP Community Katowice
entry.content:
Magdaleno,

Miałem ten sam problem z dołączeniem do grupy, gdy próbowałem dołączyć w ten sam dzień co założenie konta. Dzień później było już ok 😉

Pozdrawiam,

Krzysiek

entry.title: Re: How to use OpenTelemetry for SAP Cloud Foundry / Clou...
entry.content:
Hello BarcDe,

I am doing a Proof of Concept to explore OpenTelemetry of SAP Cloud ALM for BTP Cloud Foundry environment, for example, to monitor custom application deployed on it.

I reached to the same SAP Documentation and facing same issue as you. Do not understand what exactly is to be done. I have a custom sample CAPM based application deployed on my Cloud Foundry Subaccount, but this steps mentioned here are too vague to follow.

https://support.sap.com/en/alm/sap-cloud-alm/operations/expert-portal/data-collection-infrastructure.html

I have another question open here on community.sap.com:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/doing-a-proof-of-concept-for-btp-application-monitoring-with-sap-cloud-alm/ba-p/13703181#M167525

From your question, I realised some other steps which were not documented, at least I did not find it.

Would it be possible for you to share stepwise procedure which you described?

  1. Inserted the destination.
  2. Created a service key in the CALM subaccount.
  3. Set up the Alert Notification Service in the CF subaccount.
  4. Added the Cloud Foundry subaccount to the scope selection.
  5. Activated monitoring.

Warm regards,

Amey

entry.title: Re: SAC - model update types
entry.content: Hi, indeed a good blog. Question/issue: I have a master data table which i try to refresh using "Clean and replace selected version data". In this table i have one bigger dimension table which attached several properties to a user name. however it seems like, although the refresh works, the dimension table is not refreshed. And of course i use the dimension table in my facts table. any suggestions?

entry.title: Re: Extracting Data from S4HANA ODP to SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
Hello @chandru6382 

thanks a lot for the detailed and well-explained Blog 😊

One question remaining: Are ODP datasources only consumeable via Remote Table or also via Dataflow or Replication Flow?

I think I tried this more than one year ago, and failed... and since then I have only been using CDS Views as source for these flows.

Thanks and best regards,

Martin

entry.title: Re: Efficiency in development: Test Data in development client
entry.content:
Thanks for the insight, @matt.

Do you manually take over data from the test client into your unit tests or do you have a more efficient way?
